text:
Kudos to @TheEconomist for owning it — and for always sticking their neck out, even if they got it chopped this time. 

(Plus they are right — this time — about China: it was the invisible hand that rebalanced the oil market). 

https://t.co/zqxDrYwB1y

JPMorgan, beloved by the oil bulls for its $200 warning in March, is now decidedly bearish:

"... a wave of oil is about to enter the market. And here lies the paradox. The surge in oil supply is about to collide with a market that, at least for now, simply does not need it..."

CHART OF THE DAY: The history of US energy consumption, from independence to present day. Historic milestone ahead: nat gas about to overtake oil.

(An annual treat from @EIAgov ahead of 4th of July festivities. Please, @EIA_One keep the tradition). https://t.co/TJ6deA8lNx
The full commentary from @EIAgov is here: https://t.co/rEgl1Vhghh
